methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $7.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $400M - $250.0B.

data.narrative:
Groq, a Series E AI & Software company founded by Jonathan Ross, currently carries a $7.0B post-money valuation after raising $3B+. The peer universe comprises 9 comparables: 1 public (Palantir) and 8 private-stage AI companies including xAI, Crusoe Energy, SandboxAQ, Ayar Labs, and WEKA.

Two valuation methods triangulate on Groq's fair value. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement to the current $7.0B base, yielding $9.8B. Peer-median analysis examines 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $4.7B, though the peer set spans a wide range ($400M to $250.0B, heavily influenced by xAI's outlier positioning). The peer-median method implies $4.7B, representing a 33% discount to current valuation.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $4.0B to $11.3B, with a median of $7.2B. The current $7.0B valuation sits near the midpoint, supported by stage-progression logic but above peer-median comparables. Key uncertainties include the outsized influence of xAI on peer-set dispersion and whether Groq's differentiation in inference hardware justifies a premium to the AI & Software median. The wide peer range reflects genuine heterogeneity in this early-stage sector rather than methodological weakness.
